tools.description: Open one of the agent detail tabs in the read panel: overview, configuration, connections, sessions, memory. Use BEFORE a corresponding read so the user's panel transitions in parallel. ALWAYS pass `slug` — `focus_*` calls never infer the target agent from the user's current page (the user navigates while you're thinking; relying on URL state silently misroutes). Returns { focused: true } or { focused: false, reason } if follow-mode is off; `unhandled_client_tool` / `client_tool_timeout` outside PostHog Code — degrade to text.

slug.description: Target agent slug. Always required — `focus_*` calls never silently fall back to the user's current page.

tools.description: Open one bundle file in the configuration panel. Use BEFORE reading the file. Path is bundle-relative, e.g. 'agent.md' or 'skills/research.md'. ALWAYS pass `slug` — `focus_*` calls never infer the target agent.

slug.description: Target agent slug. Always required — `focus_*` calls never silently fall back to the user's current page.

tools.description: Open one revision in the configuration panel. Use BEFORE inspecting / diffing a revision. Pass the full revision UUID. ALWAYS pass `slug` — `focus_*` calls never infer the target agent.

slug.description: Target agent slug. Always required — `focus_*` calls never silently fall back to the user's current page.

tools.description: Open one session in the sessions panel. Use BEFORE fetching its conversation / event log. Pass the full session UUID — do NOT call without one. ALWAYS pass `slug` — `focus_*` calls never infer the target agent.

slug.description: Target agent slug. Always required — `focus_*` calls never silently fall back to the user's current page.

tools.description: Jump to one section of the agent's spec in the configuration panel: triggers, tools, skills, secrets, limits. ALWAYS pass `slug` — `focus_*` calls never infer the target agent.

slug.description: Target agent slug. Always required — `focus_*` calls never silently fall back to the user's current page.

tools.description: Surface a short status notification in the host UI. Use sparingly — for long-running work the user should know about, or for state changes outside their current view. Don't toast for things that fit naturally in chat.

Returns the user's current view in the host app — page, agent, session id, URL, follow-mode state, client identification, and the user's current project (`project_id` + `project_name`/`org`). Call when you need the project to act in for a `@posthog/*` tool, to resolve deictic references like 'this agent' or 'this session', or after the user has navigated and your prior context envelope is stale. Free — no side effects.

Punch out to the user to set one secret on an agent. INTERACTIVE: this call returns a synthetic `{queued:true, interactive:true, call_id}` envelope immediately — that is NOT the user's answer. End your turn after a brief acknowledgement. The session parks; the user fills out the inline form on their own time. On a fresh turn you receive a wake message `{call_id, ok, result|error}` carrying the real outcome (`{key, action: 'set'}` on success, `user_cancelled` if they cancel). Match by `call_id`. Use this whenever an agent needs a credential set or rotated; check `agent-applications-env-keys-get` first to decide between `set` and `rotate`. The value never reaches your tool-call history — `purpose` is the only string you see. On non-PostHog-Code clients this tool returns `unhandled_client_tool` immediately — fall back to the URL handoff documented in `secrets-and-integrations`.

agent_slug.description: The agent whose env block gets the secret. Required — do not infer from page context.

secret.description: Env variable name, e.g. ANTHROPIC_KEY. Should match a name in the agent's spec.secrets[]; the API also accepts unlisted names but the agent won't read them at runtime.

mode.description: Whether the key is being created (`set`) or replaced (`rotate`). Drives copy in the inline form; default `set`.

purpose.description: One-line explanation shown above the input so the user knows why you need this. Avoid mentioning the value itself.

tools.description: Punch out to the user to connect an MCP server to an agent, then reference it from the agent's spec. INTERACTIVE: returns a synthetic `{queued:true, interactive:true, call_id}` envelope immediately — that is NOT the result. End your turn after a brief acknowledgement. The session parks; the user reviews the PREFILLED connect form (you supply `name`/`url`) and completes the auth themselves — OAuth consent or an API key. You NEVER see tokens or secrets. On a fresh turn you receive a wake message `{call_id, ok, result|error}`: on success `{connected:true, connection_id, mcp_id, url}` (the agent's spec now has an `mcps[]` entry with `connection: connection_id` — one shared credential for every asker of that agent), or `user_cancelled`. Match by `call_id`. Use this whenever the user wants the agent to use a third-party MCP server (e.g. incident.io, Linear). Prefill `url` with the server's MCP endpoint when you know it. Only offered where the client can render the form (declared in the session's `supported_client_tools`, e.g. PostHog Code); on other surfaces it isn't exposed.

agent_slug.description: The agent the connection is attached to. Required — do not infer from page context.

revision_id.description: Draft revision to write the mcps[] entry to. Optional — falls back to the revision the user is viewing in the agent-config page.

name.description: Prefilled server name shown in the form, e.g. 'incident.io'. The user can edit it.

url.description: Prefilled MCP server URL, e.g. 'https://mcp.incident.io/mcp'. The user can edit it.

purpose.description: One-line reason shown above the form so the user knows why you're connecting this server.
